layers.name: Core
layers.description: Domain contracts, the DriftGuard facade, detector engine, state stores, and base event sinks.

layers.name: Algorithms
layers.description: Built-in Page-Hinkley, ADWIN, PSI, Kolmogorov-Smirnov, and chi-square detectors with supporting structures.

layers.name: Kafka Streams
layers.description: Kafka SerDes, topology builders, state integration, telemetry, and stream-processing error handling.

layers.name: Spring Boot Integration
layers.description: Auto-configuration, property binding, Micrometer input, and observability adapters for integrating DriftGuard.

layers.name: Testkit and Quality Gates
layers.description: Synthetic metric scenarios, benchmark runners, quality gates, and reports for evaluating detectors.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: JUnit behavioral and integration tests covering real interactions across the core, algorithms, Kafka, and Spring Boot modules.

layers.name: Configuration and Documentation
layers.description: Root Maven configuration, module manifests, scripts, fixtures, and README files supporting project development.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand DriftGuard's purpose, repository boundaries, and Maven modules. Its runtime flow provides a map for reading the implementation.

tour.title: Metric Domain
tour.description: MetricKey and MetricPoint define the stable input contract. They establish metric-stream identity and the observations processed by every detector.
tour.languageLesson: Java records and immutable value objects make domain contracts explicit and provide reliable value equality.

tour.title: Detector Contracts
tour.description: DetectorAlgorithm, DetectorConfig, and DetectorDefinition form the extension API. These contracts separate core orchestration from specific statistical implementations.

tour.title: Runtime Engine
tour.description: The DriftGuard facade forwards MetricPoint values to DriftDetectorEngine, which selects detector instances, updates runtime state, and publishes results.

tour.title: Drift Events
tour.description: DriftEvent, DriftSeverity, and DriftDirection describe detection results for consumers. Event sinks separate drift computation from alert and telemetry delivery.

tour.title: Built-in Algorithms
tour.description: Compare Page-Hinkley and ADWIN, then follow their shared support utilities. They show how one DetectorAlgorithm contract supports different statistical models.

tour.title: Kafka Topology
tour.description: KafkaDriftGuardTopologyBuilder connects input topics, SerDes, detector execution, and output events in a Kafka Streams topology, including persisted detector state.

tour.title: Spring Boot Starter
tour.description: Auto-configuration assembles core, algorithms, Kafka, and Micrometer adapters from application properties, exposing the main integration surface for applications.

tour.title: Scenarios and Quality Gates
tour.description: The testkit generates reproducible metric streams with known drift intervals, runs benchmarks, and applies quality gates to evaluate detector behavior.

tour.title: Integration Tests
tour.description: Finish with engine, Kafka topology, and Spring auto-configuration tests. They document real module interactions as executable usage examples.
